314	Depending on the project, the user's goal might be in a `.md` file, in `.lean` directly (completing the sorries), in the existing blueprints, in the references, or some combination. The blueprints should therefore all of the dependencies that are necessary to reach the goal.

--- sample literal REF in AbelJacobi ---
68:  \emph{Classical description.} The classical proof of the same statement proceeds in two parts. In positive genus (\(g(C) > 0\)), the existence and uniqueness of the factorisation come from the Pic-scheme representability theorem together with the Stein factorisation of the Abel--Jacobi morphism: see Theorem~\ref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness}, Route~A (sub-steps A.1--A.4) for the line-bundle / universal-property route via \(\Pic^0_{C/k}\), and Route~B (sub-steps B.1--B.3) for the symmetric-power / Stein-factorisation route. In genus \(0\), the same statement reduces to the rigidity theorem for \(C \to A\) --- the named declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}. This rigidity result is established \emph{over the algebraic closure \(\bar k\)}: its signature carries an \(\mathrm{IsAlgClosed}\,\bar k\) hypothesis, and it is built out of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii) over the algebraically closed base. The resulting equality of morphisms is then descended back to the original base field \(k\) by faithfully-flat descent: because \(\Spec \bar k \to \Spec k\) is faithfully flat, two \(k\)-morphisms that agree after base change to \(\bar k\) already agree over \(k\) (\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.epi\_of\_flat\_of\_surjective}). See Theorem~\ref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness}, sub-cases C.1--C.3, in particular the seven-step expansion C.2.a--C.2.g, where sub-step C.2.f (faithfully-flat descent of morphism equality from \(\bar k\) back to \(k\)) is the descent step carrying the algebraically-closed-base rigidity conclusion down to the original base field; a base-change-to-\(\bar k\) step (establishing rigidity) followed by this descent step is on the M2 critical path. The protected signature of Theorem~REF does \emph{not} assume \(C(k) \neq \emptyset\), so the genus-\(0\) argument branches on whether a \(k\)-rational point exists. When \(C(k) \neq \emptyset\), the supplied \(P \in C(k)\) feeds the pointing hypothesis of \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} directly --- no \(C \cong \mathbb P^1_k\) identification is required, sidestepping the Brauer--Severi obstruction (Brauer--Severi conics over \(\mathbb Q\) are the standard counterexample to an unconditional \(k\)-side identification). When \(C(k) = \emptyset\), the \(\mathtt{isAlbaneseFor}\) field of the genus-\(0\) witness (\cref{def:genusZeroWitness}) is vacuously true at the Lean type level: the universal quantifier \(\forall\, (P \colon \mathbf{1} \to C)\) ranges over the empty type. None of this classical content is replayed in the Lean formalisation: it is bundled into the deferred existence hypothesis Theorem~REF, whose proof is the project's single explicit foundational gap.

names=[l.strip() for l in open('/tmp/unmatched_names.txt') if l.strip()]
# build index: search for the short name (last segment) decl in lean files
# Use grep across the tree for "def/lemma/theorem/instance/abbrev <shortname>"
filecount=Counter()
unfound=[]
# Precompute: ripgrep all declarations once
import json
# We'll grep per name's last identifier
for n in names:
    short=n.split('.')[-1]
    # search for the fully-qualified or the short name as a declaration
    try:
        r=subprocess.run(['grep','-rlE',r'(^|\.| )'+re.escape(short)+r'\b','--include=*.lean','AlgebraicJacobian'],capture_output=True,text=True,timeout=30)
        files=[f for f in r.stdout.split() ]
    except Exception:
        files=[]
    if files:
        # pick the file that actually declares it
        decl=None
        for f in files:
            rr=subprocess.run(['grep','-nE',r'\b(def|lemma|theorem|instance|abbrev|structure|class|noncomputable def)\b.*\b'+re.escape(short)+r'\b',f],capture_output=True,text=True)
            if rr.stdout:
                decl=f;break
        if decl is None: decl=files[0]
        filecount[decl]+=1
    else:
        unfound.append(n)
for f,c in filecount.most_common(60):
    print(f'{c:4d}  {f}')
print('UNFOUND',len(unfound))
for u in unfound[:30]: print('   ?',u)
PY

import subprocess,re
from collections import defaultdict
names=[l.strip() for l in open('/tmp/unmatched_names.txt') if l.strip()]
filemap=defaultdict(list)
for n in names:
    short=n.split('.')[-1]
    r=subprocess.run(['grep','-rlE',r'\b'+re.escape(short)+r'\b','--include=*.lean','AlgebraicJacobian'],capture_output=True,text=True)
    files=r.stdout.split()
    decl=None
    for f in files:
        rr=subprocess.run(['grep','-nE',r'\b(def|lemma|theorem|instance|abbrev|structure|class)\b[^=]*\b'+re.escape(short)+r'\b',f],capture_output=True,text=True)
        if rr.stdout: decl=f;break
    if decl is None and files: decl=files[0]
    if decl is None: decl='??'
    filemap[decl].append(n)
import json
json.dump(filemap,open('/tmp/filemap.json','w'),indent=0)
for f in sorted(filemap,key=lambda x:-len(filemap[x])):
    print(f"{len(filemap[f]):4d}  {f}")

=== AuslanderBuchsbaum.tex head ===
\chapter{Auslander--Buchsbaum}
\label{chap:Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum}
% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean

% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-174). This chapter is the A.4.b sub-row of Route A
% (positive-genus arm of nonempty_jacobianWitness). It packages the
% Auslander--Buchsbaum formula and the corollary ``regular local ring
% \(\Rightarrow\) Cohen--Macaulay'' that A.4.a (the codim-1 extension proof for
% Albanese morphisms out of a regular projective surface) consumes. Per
% STRATEGY.md L30 this row is gated downstream on A.4.a but is independently
% startable on the Mathlib side: the algebra here is decoupled from the
% Albanese geometry and lives entirely under \texttt{RingTheory.*}. The
% target Lean file \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean}
% does not yet exist; this chapter is the prover-ready specification for
% creating it. Note that Mathlib already houses parts of the depth /
% projective-dimension API
% (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Depth.*},
% \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.RegularSequence},
% \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.ProjectiveResolution}); a Plan-Agent audit
% at the pinned commit (\texttt{b80f227}) should confirm what is present
% before the prover writes the file, since a fully Mathlib-backed
% Auslander--Buchsbaum would collapse this chapter to a re-export.

\section{Setup and motivation}
\label{sec:ab_setup}

A regular projective surface \(S\) over a field --- such as \(\mathbb{P}^1 \times \mathbb{P}^1\)
or the product \(C \times C\) of a smooth projective curve \(C\) with itself --- has a regular
local ring at every (closed) point, of Krull dimension \(2\). To extend a rational map
\(f : S \dashrightarrow A\) from \(S\) to an abelian variety across a codimension-\(2\) closed
point, one argues that the local ring \(\mathcal{O}_{S,x}\) at such a point has \emph{depth at
least} \(2\), hence a rational function with no codimension-\(1\) poles on a neighbourhood of \(x\)
extends across \(x\). The depth \(\geq 2\) inequality is exactly what the
Auslander--Buchsbaum formula gives: for a regular local ring \(R\) one has \(\text{pd}_R(R) = 0\),
so
\[
  \text{depth}(R) \;=\; \text{pd}_R(R) + \text{depth}(R) \;=\; \text{depth}(R)
\]
trivially, but combined with the regular-sequence definition of depth and the fact that a
regular local ring of dimension \(d\) admits an \(R\)-regular sequence of length \(d\) generating

content:
---
name: blueprint-writer
description: Update one blueprint chapter to reflect strategy changes, fill missing definitions or theorems, or align prose with the current Lean structure. Plan-agent-dispatched; one writer per chapter. May spawn reference-retriever mid-session when drafting reveals a missing source.
write_domain: "blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex"
read_only: false
can_spawn: true
default_enabled: false
mandatory: [dag]
dispatcher_notes: |
  - Dispatch one writer per chapter that the most recent blueprint
    review (the blueprint-review subagent in your catalog, when
    present) flagged as incomplete, lacking proof detail, or missing
    multi-route coverage.
  - Each directive must be precise: strategy context (the slice that
    matters for this chapter), required definitions/theorems (with
    enough mathematical detail to formalize), references, and
    explicit out-of-scope items. Writers do NOT speculate beyond
    what the directive lists.
  - **NEVER instruct the writer to add ``\leanok`` markers** (no "after
    writing the block, add `\leanok`"; no "verification: confirm
    `\leanok` is present"). The writer's descriptor forbids it —
    ``\leanok`` is managed by the deterministic ``sync_leanok`` phase. A
    directive that asks the writer to add ``\leanok`` puts it in a rule
    conflict; the writer must obey its descriptor, so the "verification"
    instruction will appear to fail until sync_leanok runs. Leave
    ``\leanok`` out of the writer's directive entirely.
  - **``\mathlibok`` is the exception — you MAY direct it.** When a
    declaration the chapter depends on is provided by Mathlib as-is, it
    is good practice to have the writer author an explicit *Mathlib
    dependency anchor*: a block that states the Mathlib result (in the
    project's notation), carries ``\lean{}`` naming the real Mathlib
    declaration, and is marked ``\mathlibok`` so the DAG treats it as
    done and the route's reliance on Mathlib stays visible. Name such
    anchors in the directive (statement + the Mathlib ``\lean{}``
    target). The writer marks ``\mathlibok`` ONLY on these anchors —
    never on the project's own to-be-proved declarations.
  - **Authorize the retriever in the writer's --write-domain.** If
    the chapter might need fresh source material, dispatch the
    writer with TWO globs:
      --write-domain 'blueprint/src/chapters/<chapter>.tex'
      --write-domain 'references/**'
    The writer itself only edits its assigned chapter (its prompt
    body enforces that), but the second glob authorizes a child
    reference-retriever if the writer discovers it needs one. Omit
    `references/**` only when you are confident the writer will not
    need new sources (e.g. purely cleanup edits).
  - If the strategy has multiple viable routes and one route's chapters
    are missing, dispatch a writer per route to bring all routes to
    parity before any prover work begins. Do not push provers onto a
    route whose blueprint coverage is incomplete.
  - After a significant writer round, re-run the blueprint review
    in the same iteration (the relevant entry in your catalog) to
    confirm the updated blueprint is now sufficient — do not assume
    the writer fixed everything.
  - If a writer's report contains entries under "Strategy-modifying
    findings", STOP and update STRATEGY.md before any further Lean
    work this iter. The writer is telling you the prose surfaced a
    strategy-level issue.
  - If a writer's report includes child reference-retriever dispatches,
    skim the new `references/<slug>.md` files before using the

tmpl=r"""# Blueprint-writer directive — {slug} (iter-273, DAG 1-to-1 coverage)

## Goal of this dispatch

Close the **1-to-1 Lean<->blueprint coverage debt** for chapter
`blueprint/src/chapters/{slug}.tex`. The Lean file(s) for this chapter contain
helper declarations that are **proved sorry-free in Lean but have NO blueprint
entry** (no `\lean{{}}` points at them). `leandag` lists each as an uncovered
`lean-aux` node. Your job: add ONE blueprint block per uncovered declaration so
every Lean decl in this chapter has exactly one `\lean{{}}`-pinned blueprint
entry, **and wire each new block into the chapter's dependency cone** so it is
NOT an isolated node.

This chapter covers {topic}

## The uncovered declarations to cover (add one block each)

Each name below is the EXACT Lean declaration name. Pin it verbatim with
`\lean{{<name>}}`. {pausednote}

```
{decls}
```

## How to write each coverage block

1. **Read the Lean file(s)** for this chapter to get each declaration's exact
   signature and intent:
{filelist}
   Open them and read the signature + docstring of each listed decl so your
   informal statement is FAITHFUL (right hypotheses, right conclusion). Do not
   guess from the name alone.

2. For each declaration, add a `\begin{{lemma}}` (or `\begin{{definition}}` for a
   `def`/`instance`/`structure`, `\begin{{theorem}}` only if it is a headline
   result) with:
   - a `\label{{}}` following the chapter's existing kebab convention
     (`lem:...`, `def:...`);
   - `\lean{{<exact Lean name>}}` — pin EXACTLY ONCE across the whole blueprint
     (do not duplicate a pin that already exists elsewhere);
   - a **one-to-three sentence** mathematical statement in prose (no Lean
     syntax, no tactic blocks — DAG integrity rule 7);
   - a proof block: since every listed decl is already proved sorry-free in
     Lean, write `\begin{{proof}} Proved directly in Lean. \end{{proof}}` (or one
     extra clause naming the parent result it is a sub-step of). These are
     internal helper lemmas; an external `% SOURCE` citation is NOT required
     unless the helper literally restates a Mathlib result, in which case make
     it a `\mathlibok` Mathlib dependency anchor instead (pin the real Mathlib
     `\lean{{}}` name and add `\mathlibok`).

3. **WIRING IS MANDATORY — no new isolated nodes.** Each new block must have at
   least one `\uses{{}}` edge in or out, connecting it into {parents} Determine
   the real call graph from the Lean source: if helper H is used in the Lean
   proof of an already-blueprinted result T, then add `H` to T's `\uses{{}}`
   (preferred), and/or have H `\uses{{}}` the sub-lemmas its own Lean proof
   calls. The end state must be: the chapter's public result transitively
   `\uses{{}}` all these helpers, so none is isolated. Do NOT dump 30
   edgeless "proved in Lean" blocks — that just trades uncovered-lean-aux for
   isolated-blueprint, which is equally incomplete.

4. **Fix literal `REF` placeholders in THIS chapter** while you are here:
   replace any literal "Theorem~REF", "Lemma~REF", "Definition~REF", etc. in
   the prose with a real `\cref{{<label>}}` (the surrounding `\uses{{}}` or context
   usually identifies the intended target). If you genuinely cannot identify the
   target, rephrase to remove the dangling reference rather than leave a literal
   `REF`.

## Hard constraints

- Edit ONLY `blueprint/src/chapters/{slug}.tex`.
- **Never add `\leanok`** — the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase owns it.
- Every new block has exactly one `\lean{{}}`; no broken `\uses{{}}` (every label
  you reference must exist in some chapter); purely mathematical prose.
- Do not delete or restate existing blocks; this is additive coverage plus the
  REF cleanup.

## Out of scope

- Do not touch any other chapter.
- Do not re-derive or expand the existing public-theorem proofs.
- Do not add `\leanok`.

## Report

List every block you added (label + `\lean{{}}` name), the `\uses{{}}` edges you
added to wire them in, how many literal-REF placeholders you fixed, and any decl
whose intent you could not determine from the Lean source (flag it rather than
fabricate a statement).
"""

for slug,m in meta.items():
    decls='\n'.join(fm.get(f,[]) for f in [0])  # placeholder
    declnames=[]
    for f in m['files']: declnames+=fm.get(f,[])
    decls='\n'.join(declnames)
    filelist='\n'.join('   - `%s`'%f for f in m['files'])
    pausednote=("NOTE: this chapter is on the permanently USER-paused Riemann--Roch route; "
                "these are stable, off-critical-path helpers — coverage is for 1-to-1 hygiene only."
                if m['paused'] else
                "These are stable substrate helpers under an already-blueprinted public API.")
    txt=tmpl.format(slug=slug,topic=m['topic'],decls=decls,filelist=filelist,
                    pausednote=pausednote,parents=m['parents'])
    open(f'.archon/logs/iter-273/dag-writer-{slug}-directive.md','w').write(txt)
    print('wrote',slug,len(declnames),'decls')

   'parents':"the chapter's identity-component open-subgroup theorem (\\cref{thm:identity_component_open_subgroup})."},
 'Picard_SheafOverEquivalence':{'files':['AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/SheafOverEquivalence.lean'],'paused':False,
   'topic':"the equivalence between sheaves of modules over a scheme and over an Over-category presentation: the phiOver/psiOver functors, their continuity, the forget natural iso, restrict-functor identification, and the chart-over iso.",
   'parents':"the chapter's main sheaf-over-equivalence statement."},
 'Picard_RelativeSpec':{'files':['AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelativeSpec.lean'],'paused':False,
   'topic':"pullback compatibility of the relative-Spec construction: the pullback cocone/iso construction for quasi-coherent algebras, affine-hom of the pullback projection, and the relative-Spec structure morphism.",
   'parents':"the chapter's relative-Spec functor and base-change results."},
 'RiemannRoch_OCofP':{'files':['AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/OCofP.lean'],'paused':True,
   'topic':"the line bundle O_C(P) at a closed point: its carrier set/submodule subfunctor, global-sections characterisation, the h0-h1=2 computation, local-lift and trivialisation helpers, and algebra-over-function-field instances.",
   'parents':"the chapter's O_C(P) global-sections / Riemann--Roch-at-a-point results."},
 'RiemannRoch_H1Vanishing':{'files':['AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/H1Vanishing.lean'],'paused':True,
   'topic':"H^1 vanishing via flasque resolutions: flasque => surjective on sections, the injective/short-exact sequences, Ext^1 vanishing from surjectivity+injectivity, skyscraper/constant-sheaf comparison maps, and additivity/zero/finite-limit preservation of sheaf-compose.",
   'parents':"the chapter's H^1-vanishing results."},
}

tmpl=r"""# Blueprint-writer directive --- {slug} (iter-273, DAG 1-to-1 coverage)

## Goal of this dispatch

Close the **1-to-1 Lean<->blueprint coverage debt** for chapter
`blueprint/src/chapters/{slug}.tex`. The Lean file(s) for this chapter contain
helper declarations that are **proved sorry-free in Lean but have NO blueprint
entry** (no `\lean{{}}` points at them). `leandag` lists each as an uncovered
`lean-aux` node. Your job: add ONE blueprint block per uncovered declaration so
every Lean decl in this chapter has exactly one `\lean{{}}`-pinned blueprint
entry, **and wire each new block into the chapter's dependency cone** so it is
NOT an isolated node.

This chapter covers {topic}

## The uncovered declarations to cover (add one block each)

Each name below is the EXACT Lean declaration name. Pin it verbatim with
`\lean{{<name>}}`. {pausednote}

```
{decls}
```

## How to write each coverage block

1. **Read the Lean file(s)** for this chapter to get each declaration's exact
   signature and intent:
{filelist}
   Open them and read the signature + docstring of each listed decl so your
   informal statement is FAITHFUL (right hypotheses, right conclusion). Do not
   guess from the name alone.

2. For each declaration, add a `\begin{{lemma}}` (or `\begin{{definition}}` for a
   `def`/`instance`/`structure`, `\begin{{theorem}}` only for a headline result)
   with:
   - a `\label{{}}` following the chapter's existing kebab convention;
   - `\lean{{<exact Lean name>}}` --- pinned EXACTLY ONCE across the whole
     blueprint (do not duplicate a pin that already exists elsewhere);
   - a **one-to-three sentence** mathematical statement in prose (no Lean
     syntax, no tactic blocks --- DAG integrity rule 7);
   - a proof block: since every listed decl is already proved sorry-free in
     Lean, write `\begin{{proof}} Proved directly in Lean. \end{{proof}}` (or one
     extra clause naming the parent result it is a sub-step of). These are
     internal helper lemmas; an external `% SOURCE` citation is NOT required
     unless the helper literally restates a Mathlib result, in which case make
     it a `\mathlibok` Mathlib dependency anchor instead (pin the real Mathlib
     `\lean{{}}` name and add `\mathlibok`).

3. **WIRING IS MANDATORY --- no new isolated nodes.** Each new block must have at
   least one `\uses{{}}` edge in or out, connecting it into {parents} Determine
   the real call graph from the Lean source: if helper H is used in the Lean
   proof of an already-blueprinted result T, then add `H` to T's `\uses{{}}`
   (preferred), and/or have H `\uses{{}}` the sub-lemmas its own Lean proof
   calls. End state: the chapter's public result transitively `\uses{{}}` all
   these helpers, so none is isolated. Do NOT dump edgeless "proved in Lean"
   blocks --- that trades uncovered-lean-aux for isolated-blueprint, equally
   incomplete.

4. **Fix literal `REF` placeholders in THIS chapter** while you are here:
   replace any literal "Theorem~REF", "Lemma~REF", "Definition~REF", etc. in the
   prose with a real `\cref{{<label>}}` (surrounding `\uses{{}}`/context usually
   identifies the target). If you genuinely cannot identify the target, rephrase
   to remove the dangling reference rather than leave a literal `REF`.

## Hard constraints

- Edit ONLY `blueprint/src/chapters/{slug}.tex`.
- **Never add `\leanok`** --- the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase owns it.
- Every new block has exactly one `\lean{{}}`; no broken `\uses{{}}`; purely
  mathematical prose.
- Additive coverage plus REF cleanup only; do not delete/restate existing blocks.

## Report

List every block you added (label + `\lean{{}}` name), the `\uses{{}}` edges you
added to wire them in, how many literal-REF placeholders you fixed, and any decl
whose intent you could not determine from the Lean source (flag, do not
fabricate).
"""

for slug,m in meta.items():
    declnames=[]
    for f in m['files']: declnames+=fm.get(f,[])
    decls='\n'.join(declnames)
    filelist='\n'.join('   - `%s`'%f for f in m['files'])
    pausednote=("NOTE: this chapter is on the permanently USER-paused Riemann--Roch route; these are stable off-critical-path helpers --- coverage is 1-to-1 hygiene only." if m['paused'] else "These are stable substrate helpers under an already-blueprinted public API.")
    txt=tmpl.format(slug=slug,topic=m['topic'],decls=decls,filelist=filelist,pausednote=pausednote,parents=m['parents'])
    open(f'.archon/logs/iter-273/dag-writer-{slug}-directive.md','w').write(txt)
    print('wrote',slug,len(declnames),'decls')
PY

import subprocess,re

names=[l.strip() for l in open('/tmp/unmatched2.txt') if l.strip()]
fm=defaultdict(list)
for n in names:
    short=n.split('.')[-1]

    files=r.stdout.split()
    decl=None
    for f in files:
        rr=subprocess.run(['grep','-nE',r'\b(def|lemma|theorem|instance|abbrev|structure|class)\b[^=]*\b'+re.escape(short)+r'\b',f],capture_output=True,text=True)
        if rr.stdout: decl=f;break
    if decl is None and files: decl=files[0]
    fm[decl or '??'].append(n)
import json
json.dump(fm,open('/tmp/filemap2.json','w'))
for f in sorted(fm,key=lambda x:-len(fm[x])):
    print(f"{len(fm[f]):4d}  {f}")
PY

input.command:
cd /home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
python3 - <<'PY'
import json
fm=json.load(open('/tmp/filemap2.json'))
# group AbelianVarietyRigidity consolidated (AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean + RigidityLemma.lean + the 1 AuslanderBuchsbaum-misattributed? no, keep to its files)
groups={
 'AbelianVarietyRigidity':['AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean','AlgebraicJacobian/RigidityLemma.lean'],
 'Albanese_AlbaneseUP':['AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean'],
 'Picard_QuotScheme':['AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean'],
}
meta={
 'AbelianVarietyRigidity':{'paused':False,
   'topic':"the rigidity theorem and genus-0 rational-map machinery (morphisms from rational/unirational varieties and from genus-0 curves into abelian varieties are constant; the bare-rigidity lemma and its supporting plumbing).",
   'parents':"the chapter's rigidity theorems (e.g. the rational-map-to-AV-extends / Mor(P^1,A)-constant results) and the genus-0 witness."},
 'Albanese_AlbaneseUP':{'paused':False,
   'topic':"the Albanese universal property of Pic^0/the Jacobian (Route-1, RR-free): the universal map and its uniqueness/factorisation helpers.",
   'parents':"the chapter's albanese universal-property theorem (\\cref{thm:albanese_universal_property})."},
 'Picard_QuotScheme':{'paused':False,
   'topic':"the Quot-scheme representability engine (RR-free): boundedness, valuative-criterion, reduction-to-pi_*W, and base-map helpers behind quot representability.",
   'parents':"the chapter's Quot representability results (\\cref{thm:quot_representable} and its supporting lemmas)."},
}
tmpl=open('.archon/logs/iter-273/dag-writer-Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum-directive.md').read()
# Build directive from the same structure by re-templating manually
base=r"""# Blueprint-writer directive --- {slug} (iter-273 batch-2, DAG 1-to-1 coverage)

## Goal

Close the 1-to-1 Lean<->blueprint coverage debt for
`blueprint/src/chapters/{slug}.tex`: every uncovered helper decl below is proved
sorry-free in Lean but has NO blueprint entry. Add ONE `\lean{{}}`-pinned block per
decl and WIRE each into the chapter cone (no isolated nodes).

This chapter covers {topic}

## Uncovered declarations (one block each; pin the EXACT name)

```
{decls}
```

## How to write each block

1. Read the Lean file(s) for exact signatures (do not guess from names):
{filelist}
2. Add `\begin{{lemma}}`/`\begin{{definition}}` (theorem only for headline) with a
   `\label{{}}`, a single `\lean{{<exact name>}}` (pinned exactly once project-wide),
   a 1--3 sentence prose statement (NO Lean syntax), and a
   `\begin{{proof}} Proved directly in Lean. \end{{proof}}` body. Internal helpers
   need no external `% SOURCE`; if one literally restates a Mathlib result, make
   it a `\mathlibok` anchor pinned to the real Mathlib name instead.
3. **WIRING (critical, and note this leandag quirk):** leandag builds edges ONLY
   from **statement-level** `\uses{{}}` (the `\uses{{}}` placed in the
   `\begin{{lemma}}...` statement, BEFORE `\begin{{proof}}`). A `\uses{{}}` written
   *inside* a `\begin{{proof}}` block does NOT create a graph edge. Therefore wire
   every new helper by adding its label to a **statement-level** `\uses{{}}` of the
   already-blueprinted result whose Lean proof calls it (preferred), or give the
   helper its own statement-level `\uses{{}}` to the sub-lemmas it depends on. Also
   keep each `\uses{{}}` on a SINGLE line. End state: {parents} transitively
   `\uses{{}}` every new helper; zero isolated nodes
   (`leandag query --isolated --chapter {slug}` must return none).
4. Replace any literal "Theorem~REF"/"Lemma~REF"/etc. in RENDERED prose with a
   real `\cref{{}}`; leave verbatim `% SOURCE QUOTE` comment text untouched.

## Hard constraints

- Edit ONLY `blueprint/src/chapters/{slug}.tex`. Never add `\leanok`. No broken
  `\uses{{}}`. Additive coverage + REF cleanup only.

## Report

Blocks added (label + `\lean{{}}`), statement-level `\uses{{}}` edges added, REF
fixes, and any decl whose intent you could not determine (flag, don't fabricate).
"""
for slug,files in groups.items():
    declnames=[]
    for f in files: declnames+=fm.get(f,[])
    decls='\n'.join(declnames)
    filelist='\n'.join('   - `%s`'%f for f in files)
    m=meta[slug]
    txt=base.format(slug=slug,topic=m['topic'],decls=decls,filelist=filelist,parents=m['parents'])
    open(f'.archon/logs/iter-273/dag-writer-{slug}-b2-directive.md','w').write(txt)
    print('wrote',slug,len(declnames))
PY

~165 new 1-to-1 helper-coverage blocks across nine chapters (Albanese
AuslanderBuchsbaum/CodimOneExtension, RiemannRoch WeilDivisor/OCofP/H1Vanishing,
Picard FGAPicRepresentability/IdentityComponent/SheafOverEquivalence/RelativeSpec)
and a further batch-2 set is landing in AbelianVarietyRigidity, Albanese
AlbaneseUP, and Picard QuotScheme. These are "Proved directly in Lean" helper
entries pinned 1-to-1 to previously-uncovered `lean-aux` declarations.

## What I need from you

1. Your standard per-chapter completeness + correctness checklist (complete?
   correct? proofs detailed enough? Lean targets well-formulated?), covering
   ALL chapters — the cross-chapter view is the point.

2. **Special attention to the new coverage blocks:** verify the one-line
   informal statements are FAITHFUL to the Lean signatures (not fabricated from
   the decl name), that each is wired into its chapter cone via a
   **statement-level** `\uses{}` (leandag ignores proof-block `\uses{}`), and
   that no new block duplicates an existing `\lean{}` pin.

3. Your `### Dependency & isolation findings` section: each broken/missing
   `\uses{}` and each isolated node tagged `wire-up` / `remove` / `keep`. The
   live leandag shows 3 isolated blueprint nodes — confirm they are the
   reviewer-certified isolation-EXEMPT set (`lem:S3_sep_2_*`, `lem:S3_pi_2_*`,
   `lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W`) and flag any NEW isolation.

4. The active-route `\lean{}` name-drift list (decls named in `\uses{}`/`\lean{}`
   that no longer exist in Lean) — the review-agent's correction domain; surface
   them for the loop.

5. Any `## Unstarted-phase blueprint proposals` per STRATEGY.md phases lacking
   blueprint coverage.

## Context the DAG agent already knows (do not re-flag as new)

- 204 `lean-aux` decls remain uncovered, dominated by the TensorObjSubstrate

  1-to-1 hygiene only.

input.content:
---
name: leandag-statement-level-uses-only
description: leandag builds dependency edges ONLY from statement-level \uses{}, ignoring \uses{} inside \begin{proof} blocks
metadata:
  type: project
---

In this project's `leandag` tool, a blueprint dependency edge is created ONLY
when a label appears in a **statement-level** `\uses{}` — i.e. a `\uses{}` placed
between `\begin{lemma}` (or definition/theorem) and the matching `\begin{proof}`.
A `\uses{}` written *inside* a `\begin{proof} ... \end{proof}` block does **not**
create a graph edge and does **not** contribute to the consumed node's `used-by`
count.

Empirically verified iter-273: `lem:depth_drops_by_one` referenced at statement
level → `used-by 1`; the same labels referenced only inside a proof block
(`lem:enat_ab_inductive_combine`, `lem:projectiveDimension_ker_eq_of_surjection`)
→ `used-by 0`, hence flagged ISOLATED. Fix: add the helper's label to the
**statement-level** `\uses{}` of the consumer (this also correctly encodes
formalization ordering — the helper must be done before its consumer).

Consequences for DAG work:
- When wiring helper-coverage blocks to kill isolation, put the `\uses{}` at the
  consumer's statement level, never only in its proof.
- A blueprint block whose proof sketch sits *inside the statement body* with no
  `\begin{proof}` environment and which is `[sorry]` in Lean reads as ∞ effort
  (no informal proof) — wrap the sketch in `\begin{proof}...\end{proof}`.
- Multi-line `\uses{}` is fine (leandag parses across newlines); the
  statement-vs-proof distinction is the real gotcha, not line-wrapping.

See [[leandag-mathlibok-anchor-unmatched-is-expected]].

---
name: leandag-mathlibok-anchor-unmatched-is-expected
description: A genuine \mathlibok external anchor always shows as unmatched/needs-lean in leandag — not a fabricated citation
metadata:
  type: project
---

The blueprint's single `\mathlibok` anchor `thm:finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed`
(`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed}`) has been flagged
TWICE (iter-270, iter-273) by the blueprint-reviewer as a "fabricated `\mathlibok`
citation — Mathlib does not provide this name / it is UNMATCHED." This is a
**recurring FALSE POSITIVE**.

Fact (verified iter-273): the theorem IS real —
`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Proper.lean:154`
`theorem finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed`. The project uses it directly
(e.g. `exact finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed k f` in
`Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean:397`; `Cohomology/.../Carriers.lean:343`).

Why leandag/reviewer mislabels it: a `\mathlibok` anchor's `\lean{}` points at a
declaration OUTSIDE the project source tree, so leandag's `unmatched`/`needs-lean`
lists it by design (it only matches against project `.lean` files). That is the
EXPECTED signature of a genuine external Mathlib anchor, not evidence of
fabrication. Do not "fix" it by removing `\mathlibok` or repinning.

If a future reviewer raises this again, re-confirm the Mathlib path and dismiss.
See [[leandag-statement-level-uses-only]].

## The leandag wiring quirk I discovered (and the ∞ regression)

Empirically (re)established this iter and saved to project memory: **leandag
creates dependency edges ONLY from statement-level `\uses{}`** — a `\uses{}`
inside a `\begin{proof}` block contributes no edge and no `used-by`. Three

block; `archon dag-query gaps` back to 0 of 0.
